title: Lesson 360
transcript: All right, last section in this domain, for example, that deals with how we protect, obviously, assets really by using asset classification systems. We just finished talking about, for example, onion networks and how they can be providing some really good security across networks by adding layers of security based on the number of hops, for example, that uh you know some data needs to go through in this section because it specifically appears in the exam outline we're going to talk about something called pruning or information pruning for example And what this refers to is something called information obfuscation. A hard word for me to say, but if you look up the word obfuscation, it means to hide. So there may be a requirement for us to be able to hide certain sensitive information based on its value and whoever is seeing, for example, portions or variations of that information. All right, so what you're seeing on this slide here are ways that we can typically use in order to obfuscate or hide data. And by the way, anytime you see a slide just like this one, it's not meant to be an all-inclusive complete list. We're just giving you examples because you can probably come up with other methods that we can use to obfuscate data like masking and so on. But these are some of the methods that the industry uses like concealing data and fabricating data and trimming data and encrypting data as well. They're all obfuscation methods. But the one that we're going to specifically mention in this video is pruning. data because people have seen it obviously appear on the exam so here's the perfect definition of this the whole purpose of information pruning or data pruning for example is to remove sensitive data so that it cannot be seen by whoever is viewing, for example, that record from a database, but the attributes will still appear on the screen. So, for example, if you're looking up a customer record, It'll portray certain information. Maybe there's a field as part of that record that you are not authorized to see. Let's say what that customer paid for a specific product. Everything else is on the screen. but the only thing that you would not be able to see is what that customer paid for that product. Although the field would still appear on your screen, but it would be blank, right? So they've pruned the information that is sensitive from your screen so that you cannot see it. That's the definition of specifically information pruning.
